event: Allegory of March - Triumph of Minerva
description: A large fresco divided into multiple registers showing a complex courtly scene. The lower portion features elegantly dressed Renaissance figures, horses with riders, and attendants before an architectural backdrop with arches and a castle. Figures in elaborate costumes stand in groups. The upper left shows a separate scene with figures on a platform or scaffold. The right side features an ornate archway framing nude classical figures. The overall composition is rich and densely populated, with warm earth tones and the faded quality of aged fresco.

_meta.title: Allegory of March - Triumph of Minerva and Sign of Aries. Frescos in Palazzo Schifanoia (detail)
